title: Ensure that the admission control plugin AlwaysPullImages is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin AlwaysPullImages is set
input_row.rationale: Setting admission control policy to AlwaysPullImages forces every new pod to pull the required images every time. In a multi-tenant cluster users can be assured that their private images can only be used by those who have the credentials to pull them. Without this admission control policy, once an image has been pulled to a node, any pod from any user can use it simply by knowing the image’s name, without any authorization check against the image ownership. When this plug-in is enabled, images are always pulled prior to starting containers, which means valid credentials are required.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --enable-admission-plugins argument is set to a value that includes AlwaysPullImages .
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --enable-admission-plugins parameter to include AlwaysPullImages . --enable-admission-plugins=...,AlwaysPullImages,...
input_row.impact: Credentials would be required to pull the private images every time. Also, in trusted environments, this might increase load on network, registry, and decreases speed. This setting could impact offline or isolated clusters, which have images pre-loaded and do not have access to a registry to pull in-use images. This setting is not appropriate for clusters which use this configuration.
input_row.default_value: By default, AlwaysPullImages is not set.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be verified through the Kubernetes API by checking the flags set for the kube-apiserver.
- The audit command provided in the control suggests using a command that can be executed on the Control Plane node, which aligns with querying the API server configuration.
- The requirement to check for the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument indicates that this is a standard Kubernetes service property that can be validated through the API.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_admission_plugins_required_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to retrieve the configuration of the kube-apiserver.
- The specific command to check the admission plugins would be:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'
  ```
- This command retrieves the arguments passed to the kube-apiserver container, which includes the admission plugins.
- The expected configuration should include `AlwaysPullImages` in the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The output contains `--enable-admission-plugins=...,AlwaysPullImages,...`
  - **Fail:** The output does not contain `AlwaysPullImages` in the admission plugins list.
